title: Borsk Fey'lya
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{MultipleIssues|citation|updatecontent=[[Star Wars: Invasion]]}}
{{NPOV}}
{{Character
|type=New Republic
|image=[[File:BorskFeylya-NEC.jpg]]
|name=Borsk Fey'lya
|homeworld=[[Kothlis/Legends|Kothlis]]<ref name="HESB">''[[Heir to the Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref>
|birth=[[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]] or earlier,<ref name="HESB" /> Kothlis<ref name="NEGC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref>
|death=[[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]<ref name="SbS">''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]''</ref>
|species=[[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]]<ref name="HESB" />
|gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]<ref name="HESB" />
|pronouns=He/him<ref name="HESB" />
|height=1.4 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]<ref name="HESB" />
|mass=
|hair=[[Color/Legends|Cream]]<ref name="HESB" />
|feathers=

|skin=

|parents=
|partners=
|siblings=

|affiliation=*[[Bothan Spynet/Legends|Bothan Spynet]]<ref name="IH">''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''</ref>
*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]<ref name="HESB" />
*[[Alliance of Free Planets]]<ref name="TBSB">''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]''</ref>

|masters=
|apprentices=

{{Quote|He lives in a world of shifting alliances. Borsk does what Borsk needs to do, at any given moment, to benefit Borsk. And he's so jaded by that personal philosophy that he thinks everyone else plays by the same rules.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="vp">''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]''</ref>}}
'''Borsk Fey'lya''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] whose career spanned much of the history of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. A political activist from an early age, Fey'lya's prodigious skill at manipulation and strategy enabled him to emerge as a leading figure in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and retain a base of political power throughout the various crises of the fledgling New Republic. Fey'lya's ultimate ascension to power was secured in [[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]], and he led the government as its [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]'s [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. In the waning days of the New Republic, as the aliens [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|conquered]] Coruscant around him, Fey'lya strapped a bomb to his chest and detonated it, taking over twenty-five thousand Yuuzhan Vong warriors with him to his death and securing his place in history as a Bothan [[Martyrs|martyr]].

Throughout his time in the political theatre of the New Republic, Fey'lya won and lost many allies, as was his wont during his constant, ever-ambitious quest for power. His rivalry with the reputable [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]], dislike of the [[New Jedi Order]] and anti-Human sentiment led many to possess an unfavorable opinion of the Bothan, though Fey'lya's obsession in his pursuit of power was mirrored in his belief that his actions should enable the best for the most. His fear of the usurpation of his power was reflected in his antagonistic attitude to the armed forces, whom he perceived as a contender to politics, and, having failed to assume control of the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic military]] during the [[Thrawn campaign]], the Bothan would consistently undermine its reputation, most notably during the early days of the Yuuzhan Vong War when it served his interests to refuse to acknowledge the existence of the alien threat.

==Biography==
===Early life===
{{Quote|Fey'lya, from all accounts, had a distinguished rise to power.|Voren Na'al|<ref name="DFRSB">''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]''</ref>}}
[[File:BorskFeylya-NEGTC.png|left|thumb|200px|Borsk Fey'lya]]
Borsk Fey'lya grew up on [[Kothlis/Legends|Kothlis]], a [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] colony [[Planet/Legends|world]], to the Fey'lya branch of [[Clan Alya]]. At a young age, he realized he had little aptitude in piloting and athletics, so he turned to politics. Since he was from "second-rate" Kothlis instead of the affluent Bothan [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]], Fey'lya felt he had to push himself harder than others. He was known to have been involved in several business schemes prior to the start of his political career, including shipping, mining and merchandising, though he did not pursue such projects for long before turning to politics.<ref name="dfr">''[[Dark Force Rising]]''</ref> With a few legitimate land deals on Bothawui, a number of Bothans latched onto him, and he found himself with a good deal of political support. Eventually, he was elected [[Chief Councilor]] of the [[Combined Bothan Clans|Bothan Council]]. However, Fey'lya feared the anti alien bias of the Empire and joined the [[Bothan Spynet/Legends|Bothan Spynet]].

Rising to a high rank in the Bothan Spynet<ref name="IH" /> and becoming a prominent [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]],<ref name="SESB" /> Fey'lya accumulated [[Politics/Legends|political]] power while his rivals were thwarted by manufactured scandals and their own lack of charisma. He soon commanded a faction of fellow Bothans who latched on to his success, and at this point, he began to pursue the goal of achieving control over the entire [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Seeing the end of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] rule as an inevitability, Fey'lya began making overtures toward the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="NEGC" /> The Empire took notice of Fey'lya, designating him as a high-value target for observation or arrest as a suspected political advisor to the Rebels.<ref name="IH" />

Fey'lya felt confident enough to lend the Alliance his full support after the destruction of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]],<ref name="NEGC" /> and shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="SEPG">''[[Shadows of the Empire Planets Guide]]''</ref> he led a sizeable group of Bothans<ref name="HESB" />–including nearly 65 percent of Clan Alya's members–in fleeing Kothlis and joining the Alliance. The Bothans who followed Fey'lya into the Alliance also included most of [[Clan Ojia]]; [[Torel Fey'jia]], an associate of Fey'lya who provided him with information and contacts, stayed behind and served as an inside connection for Fey'lya.<ref name="SEPG" /> Fey'lya's Bothans served primarily in support and reconnaissance,<ref name="DFRSB" /> giving the Rebellion an advantage in espionage,<ref name="NEGC" /> but would occasionally see action as the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|war]] intensified.<ref name="DFRSB" /> Fey'lya himself immediately began to seek power and position upon joining the Alliance and made deals with numerous beings, making it abundantly clear that he expected a high position in the fledgling political system being arranged by [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]].<ref name="HESB" />

The Bothans' entrance to the Alliance roughly coincided with that of [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] and the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]]–who, unlike the Bothans, possessed a fleet of [[Star Cruiser/Legends|star cruisers]]. Mutual enmity arose between Fey'lya and Ackbar, who regarded him as ambitious and self-serving and made no effort to conceal his assessment of the Bothan.<ref name="HESB" /> Fey'lya, for his part, felt that the contributions of his people and himself were undervalued, while the Mon Calamari had been granted a position of undue importance.<ref name="DFRSB" /> Dismissing Fey'lya as an opportunist, Ackbar would pay little attention to him over the next few years.<ref name="HESB" /> Nevertheless, Fey'lya became one of the most important figures in the Alliance, especially after [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]] left the group.<ref name="NEGC" />

===With the Bothan Spynet===
{{Quote|<s></s>''Liberty'', this is Fey'lya. What's going on here?|Borsk Fey'lya|audio=BorskFeylya.flac|<ref name="xwa">''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]''</ref>}}
[[File:BorskFeylya-AgentsOfDeception.jpg|thumb|220px|Fey'lya during the Galactic Civil War]]
Fey'lya quickly rose through the ranks of the Bothan Spynet. Concurrently with his activities for the Bothans, Fey'lya hoped to achieve recognition for enlisting the support of former Senator and Alliance pariah Garm Bel Iblis, who had left the Alliance to Restore the Republic following a split with Mon Mothma. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], following Bel Iblis' successful [[Raid on Tangrene|destruction]] of an [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ubiqtorate/Legends|Ubiqtorate]] base at [[Tangrene]], Fey'lya entered into a long running, secretive campaign to draw Bel Iblis back into the ranks of the resistance, while continuing to champion his species' support of the Alliance to Restore the Republic. His attempts to secure Bel Iblis' support remained largely unsuccessful, even though he continued to supply the rogue [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]], and at some point before [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] he mobilized several Bothan vessels to [[Battle of New Cov|defend]] the former Senator's interests on [[New Cov/Legends|New Cov]] in the [[Churba sector/Legends|Churba sector]].<ref name="dfr" /> And yet, Fey'lya led a delegation to meet with elements of the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] shortly before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]],<ref name="xwa" /> without having attained Bel Iblis' alliance, although he continued to extend clandestine overtures to the Senator over the years.<ref name="dfr" /> Operating out of the [[Tal'cara (Dreadnaught-class)|''Tal'cara'']], Fey'lya was escorted by famed pilots [[Olin Garn]] and [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace Azzameen]] to the [[MC80 Liberty Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 Star Cruiser]] [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']], though his shuttle was [[Mission to meet Bothan delegation|attacked]] by [[Falcon's Nest mercenaries|mercenaries]] purportedly hired by the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustans]]. The ever cautious Bothan immediately suspected foul play, although he and his delegation were successfully defended. The attack was in fact a ruse to drive a wedge between the members of the alliance, but the Sullustans were exonerated when the treachery of the mercenaries was uncovered.<ref name="xwa" /> The Bothan Spynet, along with Fey'lya, went on to lend its full support to the rebel cause, with Borsk personally involved in the appropriation of the [[Death Star II plans]].<ref name="sote">[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]]</ref> However, Fey'lya's obsession with the bravery of the [[Martyrs]], the Bothan spies who perished obtaining information on the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]], would still be utilized by him as a political tool long after the defeat of Emperor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]].<ref name="ir">''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]''</ref>

===Councilor Fey'lya===
{{Quote|Borsk Fey'lya is quite persuasive…he has Mon Mothma's ear in many things.|Gial Ackbar|<ref name="rs">''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]''</ref>}}
====Rivalry with the military====
[[File:BorskFeylya-XWRS3.png|thumb|left|230px|Fey'lya on the Provisional Council]]
Fey'lya's rise to power might have ended with Ackbar's dismissal of him, but the retrieval of intelligence that revealed the existence and location of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] by Bothans who belonged to his faction assured his place in history.<ref name="HESB" /> Fey'lya signed the [[Declaration of the Alliance of Free Planets]]<ref name="TBSB" /> and, in the aftermath of the [[Bakura Incident]], attended a briefing given to the Alliance council by the [[Historian/Legends|historian]] [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]]. While Fey'lya believed the [[Ssi-ruu]]k were a threat, he disagreed with Na'al over the magnitude of the threat, asserting based on available data that the Ssi-ruuk were likely to remain in their own region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="Report">{{WEGCite|story=Report to the Provisional Council of the Alliance of Free Planets|book=The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook}}</ref> Within four weeks of the release of the Declaration of the Alliance of Free Planets,<ref name="TBSB" /> Fey'lya signed the [[Declaration of a New Republic/Legends|Declaration of a New Republic]];<ref name="HESB" /> to indicate that he was signing as a representative of Clan Alya, he did not include his personal signet under the clan sigil.<ref name="MBC">''[[Star Wars Miniatures Battles Companion]]''</ref>

Fey'lya became a member of the New Republic's [[New Republic Provisional Council|Provisional Council]], as well as its [[Inner Council (New Republic)|Inner Council]]; as such, Bothans looked to him as a source of guidance. As Councilor, Fey'lya proved to be an effective administrator when he abstained from games of political one-upmanship. Nevertheless he sought to debate Ackbar on every decision or proposal the Calamarian brought to the Council, and he did his utmost to pass legislation that benefited the Bothans.<ref name="HESB" /> Fey'lya continued to administer some of his businesses while serving in the New Republic,<ref name="DFRSB" /> but he usually attended to such ventures in his own time, and they rarely reflected on his Council duties. Much to the chagrin of Ackbar–as well as other members of the new government, such as [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]–Fey'lya became one of Mon Mothma's most-trusted advisors.<ref name="HESB" />

Six months after the Battle of Endor, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] of [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] received a report on the status of Imperial military forces from [[Dirk Harkness]], a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] who had unexpectedly disappeared after Endor. Councilor Fey'lya expressed skepticism to Cracken regarding the accuracy of the so-called [[Harkness Report]] and questioned the motives of its author, who he suspected was providing misinformation and attempting to divert the Republic's energies.<ref name="J2">{{JournalCite|2|Recon & Report: The Journey to Coruscant}}</ref> Fey'lya was also concerned that Cracken's informant [[Platt Okeefe/Legends|Platt Okeefe]] could be a security risk due to Okeefe's association with the [[Black Curs]] mercenary group.<ref name="J8">{{JournalCite|8|Black Curs Blues}}</ref> When evidence indicating that [[Trell'yar]]–one of the Bothans who had been involved in the discovery of the second Death Star–was an Imperial spy emerged, Councilor Fey'lya stated for the record that he believed the allegations against Trell'yar were false.<ref name="WBC">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']]</ref>

Fey'lya's lack of confidence in the Harkness Report would prove to be justified in at least one respect; while Harkness claimed that Imperial [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] had been deposed and forced into [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] within six months of the Battle of Endor,<ref name="J2" /> in reality Pestage was still in power<ref name="XW:M">''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: Masquerade]]''</ref> approximately eleven months after Endor. Around that time,<ref name="Omnibus">''[[Star Wars Omnibus: X-Wing Rogue Squadron Volume 3]]''</ref> Fey'lya communicated via [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] with Leia Organa, who informed him that Pestage had offered to leave [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] undefended in exchange for being granted asylum, along with dominion over twenty-five other worlds. Fey'lya expressed some uncertainty regarding the ethics of the exchange, but promised to relay news of the development to Mothma at the earliest convenience.<ref name="XW:M" /> Approximately one month later,<ref name="Omnibus" /> General Cracken informed Fey'lya and the rest of the Provisional Council of reports that Pestage had been deposed and arrested. Fey'lya assumed the deal with Pestage was defunct as a result, and reacted with disbelief when Organa proposed that the Republic should attempt to rescue Pestage.<ref name="XW:MR">''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: Mandatory Retirement]]''</ref>

Despite Fey'lya's protests, Mothma directed Ackbar and Cracken to devise strategies to retrieve Pestage from [[Ciutric IV|Ciutric]]. After Ackbar and Cracken presented their plan, Fey'lya continued to question the logic of placing New Republic forces at risk for the sake of an Imperial defector, further alleging that efforts to rescue the [[Human/Legends|Human]] Pestage could be perceived by non-Humans as an act of [[Speciesism|bias]]. Fey'lya ultimately agreed with Councilor [[Kerrithrarr/Legends|Kerrithrarr's]] argument in favor of the plan, and voted to allow the [[Evacuation of Ciutric|mission]] to proceed. Cracken later notified Fey'lya and the other Councilors that, while Republic forces consisting of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] and [[Commando Team One]] had captured Pestage, they were trapped on Ciutric due to the arrival of Imperial reinforcements. Fey'lya sided with [[Doman Beruss (Corellian)|Doman Beruss]] in opposing Leia Organa's insistence on sending additional troops to Ciutric, agreeing with Beruss that escalating the Republic's investment in the mission would only weaken the Republic.<ref name="XW:MR" />

Under Mon Mothma, Fey'lya's influence and prestige grew, his natural cunning and ambition marking him out as one of the dominant politicians of the New Republic.<ref name="rs" /> In preparation for the drive on [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]], Fey'lya advocated extending negotiations to [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]], subsequently criticizing the pilots of Rogue Squadron for allowing a freighter owned by the despot to escape. Ever quick to undermine and belittle the actions of the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic military]] due to the threat to his species' status as martyrs he felt it posed, Borsk would enter into a long-running feud with [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and his squadron, though his quarrel truly lay with the charismatic [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]].<ref name="wg">''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]''</ref> Any action Fey'lya could take to bolster his and the Bothans' own prestige, he would, and this drive would reveal itself in the years to come.<ref name="ir" /><ref name="wg" /> The Bothan senator saw his own personal gain as one and the same with both the gain of his people and the gain of the New Republic. Any other member of the Alliance he saw as possessing too much power or influence, such as the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]], he would attempt to weaken.<ref name="htte">''[[Heir to the Empire]]''</ref> Fey'lya took constant steps to chip away at Ackbar's power base, exemplified during the drive on Coruscant, when he organized a Council meeting on the planet of [[Noquivzor]]; reminiscent of Bothawui, but extremely uncomfortable for his opponent, the Mon Calamari Admiral.<ref name="wg" />

====Machinations on Coruscant====
{{Quote|Face it, Commander; Ackbar's lost whatever touch he ever had with the common fighting man of the alliance. Fey'lya's the only one on the council who cares about our welfare.|A New Republic dispatcher to Wedge Antilles|<ref name="htte" />}}
It was Fey'lya who called for the insertion of criminals from the prison world of [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]] onto Coruscant prior to its conquest, in order to weaken the Imperial grip on the capital, and Fey'lya who urged Ackbar to strike quickly, encouraging the Admiral to carry out his plan to conquer the planet, before inactivity could harm the Bothan's reputation. When Imperial Center [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|fell]] to the Alliance, Fey'lya retained his position of power and his seat on the Provisional Council.<ref name="wg" /> He remained an opponent of Antilles and Rogue Squadron, strongly supporting the [[Trial of Tycho Celchu|trial]] of war-hero [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]] which followed the planet's fall. The trial was in Fey'lya's interests; his fear of a Human-dominated New Republic was such that the inquisition into the affairs of a Human officer, and an [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaanian]] at that, would weaken the hold of the Humans on the new government. It was therefore only a progression of Fey'lya's logic that the [[Krytos virus]] ravaging non-humans on Coruscant was linked to an anti-alien conspiracy.

Fey'lya attempted to monopolize his power and retain his position by dominating the manner by which the New Republic responded to the virus' threat. His rivalry with colleagues Leia Organa and Ackbar continued to deepen as he felt they opposed his motions and practices, and thus his antagonism toward Rogue Squadron, which he perceived to be linked to Ackbar's power base, grew more outright. He attempted to intervene in the squadron's affairs, approaching [[Asyr Sei'lar]], a Bothan ace in the unit. Fey'lya asked the younger Bothan to draft a report which stated that the destruction of a bacta convoy at the hands of Warlord Zsinj was due to errors made by Humans in the New Republic government, thus fuelling the conspiracy theory he sought to propagate; that alien species were being deliberately denied aid by Humans in the fight against the virus. In return he promised Sei'lar he would ease xenophobia among leading Bothans, and claimed that he was guarding her interests. In Asyr, Borsk saw a great asset. However she would disappoint him on this occasion by failing to carry out her task. Rogue Squadron became the heroes of the Coruscant campaign and the fight against the Krytos virus. Fey'lya's efforts to discredit Antilles' unit were thwarted. To the Bothan's embarrassment, he was powerless to prevent Antilles from leading his squadron against Ysanne Isard on [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]]. He had intended to leave the former director of Imperial Intelligence to her own devices, as he did not wish the New Republic to be seen to involve itself in the internal affairs of non-member worlds. With Rogue Squadron's resignation ''en masse'' from the New Republic and their disregard for his authority, Borsk had earned himself a series of long term enemies in men such as Antilles, Celchu, [[Gavin Darklighter]] and [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]].<ref name="tkt">''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]''</ref>

At some point during Fey'lya's tenure as Bothan leader, [[Brandis Turgah]]—a thief masquerading as a technician in the New Republic fleet—was captured while attempting to steal from a group of Bothans and brought before Fey'lya. Seeing the value in having a thief and informer in the New Republic fleet, Fey'lya released Turgah under the condition that he would regularly report to the Bothans with interesting news and classified data that would otherwise be unavailable to them. When the Bothan delegation subsequently learned of the formation of a task force to pursue [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], Fey'lya arranged for Turgah to join the force.<ref name="DSC">''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]''</ref>

====Moving against Ackbar====
{{Quote|There's no doubt at all that Councilor Fey'lya will play his part beautifully. As beautifully as if we were pulling his strings directly.|Thrawn|<ref name="dfr" />}}
[[File:BorskFeyLya-TLC5.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Fey'lya during the Thrawn crisis]]
Fey'lya's chance to discredit and remove Ackbar as a rival would come two years later, during the depredations of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] strategist who emerged from the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] to lead the fight against the New Republic. Fey'lya soon suggested that the [[Destruction of the Elomin task force|loss]] of an [[Elomin/Legends|Elomin]] task force was emblematic of Ackbar's lack of ability as Supreme Commander of the military. However, Fey'lya soon made another enemy; he clashed with [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] over the incorporation of smugglers into the New Republic and a failed diplomatic mission to [[Bimmisaari/Legends|Bimmisaari]]. Many of his actions were carried out to undermine Ackbar, and his position as a member of the [[Inner Council (New Republic)|Inner Council]] allowed him to do this all too often. His campaign against the Admiral did not go unnoticed, however. Although much of the military had begun to see Fey'lya as a viable leader, leading politicians such as Leia Organa Solo began to realize that Borsk had his sights set not only on Ackbar's position as Supreme Commander, but also the office of [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] itself.<ref name="htte" /> Her fears were, a decade and a half later, eventually proven correct. Fey'lya's road to power would be a lengthy one.<ref name="vp" />

Following the [[Battle of Sluis Van]], Fey'lya's faction accused Ackbar of [[Treason/Legends|treason]]. Money had been planted in the Admiral's accounts, in fact by Thrawn himself in his ploy to destabilize the New Republic government, and Fey'lya used the seeming coincidence to his advantage to finally obtain victory over his opponent as the Mon Calamari war hero was placed under house arrest<ref name="dfr" /> and scheduled for trial. Fey'lya's actions were only to divide the New Republic; Ackbar still possessed a firm support base in the military which threatened to clash with Fey'lya's bloc. For the moment, however, the Bothan's star continued to rise.<ref name="htte" /> The Council, at the Bothan's persuasion, allowed him to take over the running of several of Ackbar's internal security duties, though the politician did not push his luck, and remained relatively quiescent, until a hearing on the Battle of Sluis Van where he made his move. Having arranged the hearing through Mon Mothma, Fey'lya led the unofficial questioning of Captain Han Solo, passing up on the opportunity to criticize Solo on his tactics at Sluis Van—which had seen the New Republic vessels crippled by [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and Solo rather than be lost to the Empire—in order to lay the blame on Ackbar for dispatching the ships to the [[Sluis system]] in the first place. Fey'lya did not go so far to accuse Ackbar of treason, though he strongly suggested its presence in the Admiral's actions and openly denounced the Mon Calamari as inept. When Solo claimed at the same hearing that the Imperials were being led by a Grand Admiral, Fey'lya rebuffed the claim. However, other than his considerable support among the military, the Bothan's position was becoming increasingly tenuous. The investigation into Ackbar was still not underway, the Admiral retained a loyal support base, and Fey'lya had not presented any sound evidence against his opponent. To add to his humiliation, it was soon verified that the Imperials were being led by Thrawn, a Grand Admiral. To compound this, [[Delta Source]], a mysterious Intelligence leak in the heart of the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]], had still not been silenced; the responsibility for doing so was technically Fey'lya's. To correct the weak grounding of his fortunes, Fey'lya renewed his campaign to secure the allegiance of Senator Bel Iblis, hoping that drawing the Corellian back into the fold would garner support. His aide, [[Tav Breil'lya]], was sent to New Cov to discuss the Senator's return. Fey'lya, now faced with the opportunity to amass political support as never before, was committed firmly to his pet project, and Breil'lya was nervous enough to advocate killing Solo and Calrissian when they stumbled upon the operation on New Cov during their investigation of Fey'lya's motives.<ref name="dfr" />

====Fall from grace====
{{Dialogue|Leia Organa Solo|All this talk about being on the side of the military was nothing more than a play for political power?|Borsk Fey'lya|Don't be foolish, Councilor. What other use are soldiers to a politician?|Talon Karrde|Is that why you don't care if the men of Rogue Squadron die? Because they prefer to stay out of politics?|Borsk Fey'lya|No one cares if their enemies die. And all those who are not on my side are my enemies.|attr=Leia Organa Solo and Talon Karrde confront Borsk Fey'lya|src=<ref name="dfr" />}}
[[File:BorskFeylya-Databank.png|thumb|left|210px|Borsk Fey'lya]]
Solo and Calrissian's return to Coruscant with news that the [[Katana fleet|''Katana'' fleet]] had been found, along with the information that Thrawn was attempting to attain knowledge of its location and may well have succeeded in doing so, was fiercely disputed by Fey'lya. Once again, he denied the threat, claiming that the existence of the enigmatic fleet was Imperial disinformation. The Bothan politician continued to wait to secure Ackbar's position, though having failed to attain Bel Iblis for the New Republic, he became desperate to secure the legendary fleet himself. Meanwhile, Thrawn had managed to remove the vast majority of the [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruisers]], and was in the process of manning them with rapidly-bred soldiers, birthed from his [[Spaarti cloning cylinder]]s on [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] storehouse world of [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]]. When the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and Fey'lya's old opponent [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], along with Rogue Squadron, left Coruscant to scout the ''Katana'' fleet, Fey'lya ordered their arrest, under the pretense that they held vital knowledge on the location on the fleet and had to be sequestered. Once Leia Organa Solo had thwarted that objective, Fey'lya amassed much of his military support before grouping them aboard the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|''Nebulon''-B Escort Frigate]] ''[[Quenfis]]''. With Organa Solo and Talon Karrde onboard, and he himself in charge of the situation, Fey'lya headed out to the fleet's location in order to rid himself of his troublesome political opponents. There, Fey'lya ordered the ''Quenfis'' commanding officer, Captain [[Sarin Virgilio|Virgilio]], to arrest Solo and Antilles, along with Rogue Squadron. A heated debate between the two factions arose, during which Antilles threatened refusal of Fey'lya's demands and risked a court martial. The deteriorating situation was interrupted by the arrival of Imperial Captain [[Brandei]]'s [[Judicator/Legends|''Judicator'']], one of Thrawn's [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyers]]. To Fey'lya's amazement, Antilles defied his direct orders and engaged the Imperial drops ships as they attempted to fight off the New Republic presence.<ref name="dfr" />

Rather than risk his person, or his dedicated body of military support, for the lives of those he had come to arrest, Fey'lya ordered the retreat of the ''Quenfis''. When the Princess Leia Organa Solo pleaded with him to remain and protect her husband, brother and friends, Fey'lya drew a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] on the pregnant woman so as to silence her protests. Claiming that Organa Solo had to recognize the interests of the New Republic above her own, the Bothan failed to realize that the Force-sensitive had turned on the frigate's internal and external communications. Fey'lya began to find himself antagonized by Karrde, who maneuvered the Bothan into admitting his true feelings and designs not only for Rogue Squadron, Skywalker and the Solos, but also the entire New Republic military. Immediately, the ''Quenfis'' [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] headed to support Antilles, and Captain Virgilio arrived with his men to confine Fey'lya to his quarters. Shocked, the Bothan reluctantly surrendered his blaster and was taken away by the guards. Had Fey'lya remained on the bridge, he would have seen his political fortunes take another turn for the worse; Garm Bel-Iblis arrived to carry the fight against the Imperials, encouraged to re-join the cause not by Fey'lya, but by the earlier visit of Solo and Calrissian. The combined forces of the New Republic and Bel-Iblis' six Dreadnaughts saw off the ''Judicator'', and managed to destroy the second Star Destroyer Thrawn had dispatched, the ''[[Peremptory]]''.<ref name="dfr" />

====A slow recuperation====
Fey'lya returned to Coruscant a much diminished figure. His ploys to take personal credit for the recovery of the ''Katana'' fleet, and the re-entry of Senator Bel Iblis into the New Republic, had both failed miserably. He had lost almost the entirety of his power base among non-Bothans, as his open admission of his contempt for the New Republic military's well-being was allowed to circulate. Lastly, he had badly damaged his credibility with Mon Mothma and the rest of the Provisional Council, as the evidence supporting his treason charge against Admiral Ackbar was revealed by [[Zakarisz Ghent]] to be an Imperial fabrication, within a few days of the latter's arrival on Coruscant. In keeping with Bothan political tradition, he went into a self-imposed penance, continuing to attend Council meetings but remaining silent and withdrawn. [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] reflected irritably that this sullenness was counterproductive, when they needed every voice of counsel, even Fey'lya's, to help analyze and organize their efforts, as Thrawn launched his final offensive in earnest.

Fey'lya broke this silence only once, during a Council meeting, to unexpectedly support Organa Solo's suggestion that reinforcements be sent to support the sabotage group, which included [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], sent to destroy Thrawn's cloning facility on [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]]. Fey'lya even went so far as to suggest that the New Republic's planned [[Attack on the Bilbringi system|attack]] on the [[Bilbringi Shipyards]] be postponed in favor of sending those reinforcements. At first he claimed concern for Solo and Skywalker's lives and emphasized the importance of destroying Grand Admiral Thrawn's source of clones. However, his actions at the ''Katana'' fleet skirmish - demonstrating both an open disregard for the lives of his political opponents and a feeble grasp of military realities - led virtually everyone present to doubt his motives, and he finally admitted that there were "[[Caamas Document|things]]" in the Emperor's storehouse, besides the cloning cylinders, that could be used by Thrawn to devastating effect, against the Bothan people and the galaxy. Since he refused to elaborate, his advice was dismissed.

Fey'lya's fears persisted, however, and he was caught listening in on a conversation between Organa Solo and [[Talon Karrde]] by one of Leia's [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] bodyguards, [[Mobvekhar]]. During the ensuing conversation, he absolutely demanded that [[Mount Tantiss/Legends|Mount Tantiss]] be destroyed, though he still refused to say exactly what he feared was inside. Karrde agreed to go to Wayland, in the process forcing a payment out of Fey'lya for 70,000 credits.<ref name="tlc">''[[The Last Command]]''</ref>

===Political recovery===
{{Quote|You refer to us as 'alien' and the Princess called you 'non-human'. Why are we defined by you and in comparison to you?|Borsk Fey'lya to Doman Beruss|<ref name="tkt" />}}
[[File:BorskFeylya2.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Fey'lya during the Caamas Document Crisis]]
Fey'lya regained power as the New Republic reorganized in the wake of the resurrected Emperor Palpatine's campaign,<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|269|Fey'lya, Borsk}}</ref> and by the time of the [[Black Fleet Crisis]], Fey'lya's political fortunes had risen to a level of influence, resulting in becoming Chair of the [[Justice Council]] and a member of the [[New Republic Ruling Council|Ruling Council]]. When [[Krall Praget]] submitted a summons of no confidence against Leia, Fey'lya opposed it, feeling that the time was not right. But when [[Doman Beruss (Illodian)|Doman Beruss]] submitted his after [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo's]] capture by the [[Yevetha]], Fey'lya supported the [[Vote of No Confidence/Legends|Vote of No Confidence]] against Leia Organa Solo, and began whipping votes to secure his election as interim Chief. However, Leia declared war on the [[Yevetha]] before the vote came about, and the Senate supported her. After the crisis, Fey'lya was dispatched to [[Galantos]], one of the Republic's new member worlds. Fey'lya was never known for his patience and the [[Fian/Legends|Fian's]] attention to detail drove the Bothan to the limits of his sanity.<ref name="BtS">''[[Before the Storm]]''</ref>

Fey'lya's darkest hour was the [[Caamas Document Crisis]]. Organa Solo had taken a leave of absence, and Fey'lya was not chosen as her successor. However, [[Ponc Gavrisom]], Organa Solo's interim successor, was a close ally of his, and he served on the [[New Republic High Council]]. But the trouble was just beginning. [[Clan Alya]], Fey'lya's Bothan Clan, was accused of complicity in the devastation of [[Caamas/Legends|Caamas]], and Fey'lya, as Chair of the Justice Council, was accused of letting off Bothans guilty of aiding the attack. Teaming up with Organa Solo and Gavrisom, Fey'lya managed to weather the storm, even briefly purchasing Leia's protocol droid [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], so that 3PO could search Bothan bank accounts and look for those who were paid for the devastation of Caamas.<ref name="SP">''[[Specter of the Past]]''</ref><ref name="VF">''[[Vision of the Future]]''</ref>

Later that year, Fey'lya was an invited guest at [[Wedding of Luke Skywalker and Mara Jade|the wedding]] of Luke Skywalker and [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]]. He arrived along with [[Talon Karrde]], Mon Mothma and [[Elegos A'Kla]].<ref name="U">''[[Star Wars: Union]]''</ref>

===Chief of State===
{{Quote|Chief Fey'lya. You are a poor excuse for a sentient being. I hope one day you really smell the stink in your heart and choke on the fumes.''"<br />"''You're very young. When you've accomplished a fraction of what I have for the people of this galaxy, come back and we'll talk again.|Jaina Solo and Borsk Fey'lya|<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest">''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]''</ref>}}
[[File:BorskFeylya-NJOS.png|thumb|left|150px|Chief of State Fey'lya]]
After the resignation of [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] from the position of Chief of State, Fey'lya was [[New Republic Presidential election, 23 ABY|elected]] her successor with a three-fifths majority in the first round. The Bothan brought with him his own council, which found itself in danger as the threat of the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] emerged. Thrown into the balancing act of maintaining peace within the New Republic, attempting diplomatic relations with the Yuuzhan Vong, and keeping Luke Skywalker's Jedi under his control at all times, Fey'lya made many wrong choices, particularly in light of the Yuuzhan Vong's desire for all-out war. As the war began, Fey'lya initially refused to believe that the Yuuzhan Vong even existed, and saw the destruction of Sernpidal as an elaborate plot by the Jedi and human politicians like Leia to take back control of the New Republic. He was, unfortunately, bolstered in these beliefs by several members of his [[New Republic Advisory Council|Advisory Council]], including Senators [[Pwoe]] and [[Niuk Niuv]], who believed that, with Fey'lya's election, they had finally overturned the [[humanocentrism]] that had been the Empire's standard policy and which, in their eyes, had continued (albeit unofficially) under the New Republic, under the leadership of [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] and Leia.

Fey'lya was deeply suspicious of the [[New Jedi Order]], which was at least partially understandable, as the Jedi had failed to prevent the rise of the Empire and the fact that [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] had once been a Jedi didn't help improve their image in his mind. He was hardly alone in his attitude toward them, however—over an entire generation had grown up since the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and billions of beings across the Galaxy had no memory of when the Jedi were considered champions of justice and peace. Several other New Republic politicians voiced wariness toward granting them any special treatment or support, even those in New Republic service. The prospect of a state-sponsored religion, composed of individuals with abilities far beyond that of regular beings, was, in the minds of many, an invitation to disaster, especially in light of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic's]] fall. An easier explanation, though, might have been simple jealously, and the uncomfortable fact that one of his main political rivals, [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Organa Solo]], was the sister of the Order's leader.

In the face of overwhelming evidence, he finally relented and committed the Republic to war, but his antagonism of the Jedi and the military severely harmed the overall war effort. For most of the war, Fey'lya's primary concern was the preservation of his public image, not the New Republic. After the destruction of [[Duro/Legends|Duro]], and [[Tsavong Lah]]'s ultimatum to turn over the Jedi to the Vong, Fey'lya seemed all too ready to accede, as he allowed [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] to fall the Yuuzhan Vong.

Fey'lya also seemed willing to sacrifice other worlds as long as vital planets like Coruscant and the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]—as well as all of [[Bothan Space/Legends|Bothan Space]]—were spared the invaders' wrath. At around this time, [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] [[Director of New Republic Intelligence|Director]] [[Dif Scaur]] had begun working with the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] to develop [[Alpha Red (pathogen)|Alpha Red]], a virus designed to destroy the Yuuzhan Vong at a cellular level.<ref name="DW">''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]''</ref> He did, however, also rise to the occasion when given a chance to fight against the Vong-he took over communications for the flagship "[[Ralroost]]" during the [[Battle of Ithor]] after the previous communications officer was killed, showing he did have some measure of personal courage.

===Epiphany and death===
{{Quote|History will blame me for what happened today. Don't try to tell me otherwise.''"<br />"''There are other ways of serving.''"<br />"''Perhaps for you, Princess. But not for me. Not for Borsk Fey'lya.|Borsk Fey'lya and Leia Organa Solo|<ref name="SbS" />}}
[[File:Borsk-death.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Fey'lya's final stand on Coruscant]]
Eventually, he was forced to accept that there was no way of appeasing the Yuuzhan Vong, or of halting their advance into the Core Worlds with traditional military means, and that the Jedi were the only hope for the New Republic. When [[Nom Anor]] appeared before the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]] two years into the war demanding concessions and the lives of the Jedi, Fey'lya made a complete political turn around by stating Luke Skywalker had his full support, and lashed out at Anor, calling the Yuuzhan Vong a race of cowards. But it was already too late, as the Yuuzhan Vong launched their [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|attack]] on Coruscant, forcing its evacuation in the face of overwhelming numbers. Fey'lya asked his old rival, Leia Organa Solo, to rally the New Republic to victory. Believing that he had lost everything, and that he only had a life of scorn (and worse, powerlessness) waiting for him if he lived, he stayed behind, attaching a bomb to a heart monitor and surrendering himself to the Yuuzhan Vong in hopes of taking [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] with him. The Yuuzhan Vong leader saw through his plan and sent [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Romm Zqar]], a subordinate, instead. Zqar attempted to force his surrender, and killed him when he refused. This detonated the bomb, killing Zqar and over twenty-five thousand Yuuzhan Vong [[Warrior caste|Warriors]], and destroying almost half of the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]], including the data towers, thus preserving some of the Republic's most vital secrets. Although the invasion was a success for the Yuuzhan Vong, their losses were heavy, and Lah sourly remarked that they could scarcely afford to lose the twenty-five thousand warriors that Fey'lya had managed to take with him.<ref name="SbS" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|They look on Fey'lya as a kind of minor hero.''"<br />"''Huh. They didn't know him like we did.''"<br />"''And maybe we didn't know him as well as we should have.|Jacen Solo and Ganner Rhysode|<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Traitor">''[[The New Jedi Order: Traitor]]''</ref>}}
Whether or not this perfidious action redeemed him is questionable (indeed, some argued that he was irredeemable), Borsk Fey'lya essentially died a hero. To both his people and the New Republic at large, he had become a modern day [[Martyrs|martyr]], and his death inspired the Bothans, for the first time in centuries, to declare a state of [[ar'krai]]—total war on the Yuuzhan Vong by the Bothan people. In addition to this, the Yuuzhan Vong also respected him for his actions and believed him to be a minor hero.<ref name="DW" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Consider Borsk Fey'lya, the Bothan diplomat who rose to become Chief of State of the New Republic. He seemed to have a personal grudge against the Jedi, but ultimately, he was just a single-minded politician out to champion his species and homeworld.|[[J.D. Wiker]]|<ref name="Master of Evil">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/livingforce/swlf20040318master|text=Only a Master of Evil}}</ref>}}
Borsk Fey'lya first appeared in [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|''The Thrawn Trilogy'']], though he is established in [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] to have played a role in the Rebel Alliance since the defection of [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]] shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].

"Borsk" is [[Bothese]] for proud.<ref name="GCG">''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

===Non-canon content===
Sometime before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Borsk Fey'lya was the leader of [[Deep Ear]], a faction of the [[Bothan Spynet/Legends|Bothan Spynet]]. The group was responsible for procuring the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star's]] plans aboard the ''[[Suprosa]]'' but were later captured by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces on the planet [[Gellefon]]. Borsk managed to elude capture and met up with his alliance contact, a [[Mikoan]] known to him as [[Jade (mercenary)|Watcher]], and informed her of what had happened. The two of them, along with a group of rebels who were sent to help, helped free the members of Deep Ear from [[Pinnacle Keep]] and escaped the planet to meet up with [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] at [[Keff Base]] in the [[Sullust system/Legends|Sullust system]].
